name: maritime_safety_water_allocation_model_oakline_rig_utl
premise: A Maritime Safety response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving maritime safety water allocation model oakline rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Soren Navarro
agents.identity: I am Soren Navarro, accountable for a defensible call in Maritime Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Sana Kaur
agents.identity: I am Sana Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Maritime Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Soren Navarro must decide under rights vs utility. What does Soren Navarro do?
